entry.title: Re: SF Compensation - Executive Review: Filter Option For...
entry.content:
Hi @Ajnos 

After review with our product team we are in the process of updating this KBA to remove the sentence stating that this is not recommended for customers. If you think the customer use case can be handled using form view, then there is no reason why you shouldn't use it.

We are also updating the steps to activate it under Resolution since it can be done directly at the template level under Plan Setup> Advanced Settings.

The change should be active within the next few days.

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite: Automating Email Alerts for Failed Interfaces in SAP CPI
entry.content: @Daggolu_PremSai  very good aproach. I like It.

entry.title: SuccessFactors New People profile - Questions
entry.content:
We are setting up the new people profile and have not been able to find any information on the following:

1. Defaulting employees to the Company Profile and not the Full Profile when they search for someone.  

2. On the Full Profile , employees with no access to see other employee's banking information in RBP see the Banking Card on Personal Information even they do not have access to other employees banking information.   Can this banking card only be given to certain groups like HR?

entry.title: Re: Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content:
@Marian_Zeis next delivery for PCE (2025) will also include Joule for developers. Dowporting will follow later this year probably. S4 OnPrem is still under discussion under which circumstances might benefit. ECC is not planned to benefit of AI. The ECC customers may have access to Joule if they use Steampunk. But the issue is that Joule is design to write ABAP Cloud compliant code and the old ECC code is generally not fit for this purpose. 

@Siarljp and @FelixOrtiz you can just try it. It's free and available. If you are a Steampunk or S4/HANA Public Cloud customer you can ask and get it at no extra cost as of today. License: In order to leverage Joule’s capabilities, you need to purchase an additional license: 8019124 – Joule for developers limited promotion, ABAP. Please check SAP Note 3571857 for details. This license is a limited promotion free of charge until September 15th 2025 and can be purchased until August 15th 2025. You may condier reading also this blog writen by @AlexanderRother : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/joule-speaks-abap/ba-p/14018226

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Application Backend not working at all.
entry.content:
@FreddyG 

I can suspect you might have connection issue with your DB or API which is not reteriving the data.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/developing-an-app-with-sap-build-apps-using-github-api-less-than-5mins/ba-p/14017891

entry.title: Re: Im not able to show potencial rating in compensation...
entry.content:
Hi @ernestogabrielm 

as far as I know only results can be brought to Compensation worksheet columns, not description.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: 🚨 SAP Build Apps – Access Denied (403 Forbidden) – S...
entry.content:
@mazenelba@mazenelba @depratihari @Sateesh7 @halley_alphonse 

Please check now, issue is resolved

entry.title: Does Basic Syntax support the Format function?
entry.content:
I am using Basic Syntax.

The following simple formula:
formula = "My Number: " & Round(1234.56,0)
yields:
My Number: 1,235.00

The Round function is rounding to zero decimals but also auto-formatting the result to add a comma and display two decimal places.

I tried:
formula = "My Number: " & Format(Round(1234.56,0), "#,##0")
but it doesn't look like Basic Syntax supports the Format function.

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@H_Ettelbrueck Following this blog we set up  authentication as follows: 

Entra ID - (OIDC)- IAS - (OIDC) - App.    This set up works .  

But for Admin Console are you suggesting  to configure Entra ID - (SAML) - IAS admin console system application in IAS?  Is it possible to have both OIDC and SAML against the same EntraID Enterprise App registration?

entry.title: Re: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License
entry.content: Indeed I've heard this myself, Volker. A nightmare scenario is for SAP to maintain SQL Anywhere as an in-house tool, and not worry about supporting outside users or welcoming new ones. If SAP no longer wished to be burdened with SQL Anywhere as a commercially available and supported product, presumably they could keep and maintain an in-house copy, while forking the commercial version to Appeon.

entry.title: Re: Two-Step Picking in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Who know if 2-step-picking can we used with basic versión EWM?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Budget Calculation
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec , thanks for your answer. We want to allocate the budget for each compensation manager in the system, as we do in the salary plan where we the custom way for the budget. More specific we add with a lookup table the budget amount and we have the total amount which is allocated as it should in the process. In the variable pay form we do the same but we face an issue: we see the budget amount but at the total we see an unexpected amount which we do not know where it comes from. We use results budget. 

Also the final amount of the bonus is 0 into the final pay out field, and we enter an amount into the override field the final pay out takes the same amount with the business calculation amount field. We use only individual calculations and we do not use business goals.

Your guideline would be helpful.

entry.title: How SAP B1 specifically in picking module create information from "bin location allocation - Issue"?
entry.content:
Hi,
I have a little question about how SAP B1 create the information from "bin location allocation - Issue" to show. I would like to recreate this using a queary, I very confuse about which table are consulting.

Thanks.

entry.title: Assignment profile not picking up some users
entry.content:
AP is not picking up some set of users who are eligible for Assignment profile rules (Country and is line manager IS yes). The affected users are same domain and group as per the AP criteria and have been lone managers for long time and have 'yes' in line managers on EC and LMS profile .

 

What could be the reason for this issue ? Please help

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard Kafka Integration Options
entry.content:
If you haven't looked at Datasphere yet, then it would be worthwhile to evaluate the Datasphere support for Kafka: 

Apache Kafka Connections

entry.title: Re: Problematic "Unnamed Object" in Fiori Elements app powered by RAP endpoint
entry.content: By desgin a key is not allowed to be empty, so you may need to set the keys to other fields or create a UUID for the entries as single key field

entry.title: Re: Enhancements for SAP Community - February 2025
entry.content: @Jelena_Perfiljeva we also have a link to the SAP Managed tag pages in the "Welcome to SAP Community!" widget on the community homepage:

entry.title: Why Event-Enabling SuccessFactors with SAP BTP Advanced Event Mesh is a Game Changer
entry.content:
Why Event-Enabling SuccessFactors with SAP BTP Advanced Event Mesh is a Game Changer

Introduction

In today’s dynamic business environment, organizations need real-time, event-driven architectures to ensure agility, responsiveness, and seamless integration across HR and enterprise systems. SAP SuccessFactors, a core HR platform, contains critical workforce data that must be efficiently shared with various applications. Traditional integration approaches, such as scheduled batch jobs and point-to-point connections, often lead to latency, complexity, and scalability issues.

By event-enabling SuccessFactors with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM), organizations can unlock a more efficient and scalable integration model, ensuring that HR events trigger immediate updates across all dependent systems.

Why Event-Enable SuccessFactors?

SuccessFactors manages key employee lifecycle events, including hiring, onboarding, job changes, and terminations. These events drive business processes across multiple enterprise applications, including payroll, IT provisioning, compliance, and security systems. Without an event-driven approach, these integrations often suffer from:

  • Delays in critical HR updates due to reliance on batch processing.
  • Increased maintenance costs caused by tightly coupled point-to-point integrations.
  • Scalability challenges when adding new consumers or modifying workflows.
  • Risk of data inconsistencies between SuccessFactors and downstream systems.

By leveraging Advanced Event Mesh (AEM), HR-driven events are instantly published and consumed by all relevant systems, ensuring real-time updates and reducing operational overhead.

Key Use Cases & Benefiting Systems

1. Employee Onboarding & IT Provisioning

When a new employee is onboarded in SuccessFactors, multiple systems need to be updated in real-time:

  • Active Directory & Identity Management (IDM) – Automatically create user accounts.
  • IT Service Management (ServiceNow, Jira, etc.) – Generate tickets for equipment setup.
  • Enterprise Access Control (Okta, Azure AD, etc.) – Assign access based on role.
  • Payroll & Benefits (SAP Payroll, ADP, Workday, etc.) – Ensure timely payroll processing.
  • Facilities & Security – Provision badges, office space, and remote access credentials.
  • Assignment of learning for job related activities and compliance
  • Notification of an onboarding mentor who can assist the new hire in navigating their new work enviroment

Studies have shown that a strong onboarding process can not only improve new hire retention but also accelerate new hire productivity.  The onboarding process extend over a heterogenous landscape of different systems which all play a vital part in bringing a new hire into the organization.  By event enabling the onboarding process, a new hire’s information can easily and securely be replicated to the appropriate subscribing systems to accelerate the onboarding process.  Event enabling onboarding can also help future proof the new hire workflow by making it easy for new systems to find and consume new hire events relevant to that system’s operations.  

2. Employee Job Changes & Role Transitions

Promotions, department changes, or location transfers require multiple updates:

  • ERP Systems (SAP S/4HANA, Oracle, etc.) – Adjust reporting structures and cost centers.
  • Expense & Travel Management (SAP Concur, Expensify, etc.) – Update approval workflows.
  • Learning & Development (SAP SuccessFactors Learning, Coursera, etc.) – Assign relevant training.
  • Workforce Planning & Analytics – Update dashboards with real-time employee movement insights.

Promotions and changes can have many effects on downstream systems. By event enabling changes in employee job roles, locations and departments organizations can make sure that the relevant subscribing systems receive the necessary information to effectively handle the change in employee master data and its effects within the organization.

3. Employee Termination & Offboarding

When an employee leaves, real-time updates help enforce security and compliance:

  • Access Management Systems – Revoke system access immediately.
  • Payroll & Benefits – Finalize last payments and remove inactive employees.
  • HR Compliance & Auditing – Log changes for compliance and governance.
  • IT & Asset Management – Trigger return of company devices and deactivate accounts.

Forbes reported that over 50% of employees who leave an organization take valuable information with them.  Offboarding an employee is an event that needs to take place in real time across a complex system landscape.  Risk of not offboarding an employee in a timely manner can lead to data loss, compliance violations and breaches of confidentially.  By event enabling the offboarding process organizations can quickly safe guard their assets by notifying all subscribing systems in real time about the offboarding event. This drives better automation in the offboarding process and reducing errors from manually replicating actions in multiple systems.

4. Real-time HR Data Sync Across Global Systems

Multinational companies often maintain regional HR systems that require synchronized data:

  • Global Payroll Hubs – Ensure compliance with country-specific payroll regulations.
  • Multilingual Employee Portals – Update employee records across various platforms.
  • HR Data Lakes & Analytics – Power real-time dashboards and predictive analytics.

Benefits of Using SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh for SuccessFactors Integration

  1. Real-Time Event Distribution – Ensures immediate propagation of HR changes across the enterprise.
  2. Decoupled & Scalable Architecture – Supports adding new consumers without disrupting existing integrations.
  3. Reliable & Guaranteed Delivery – Prevents data loss with persistent event storage and retry mechanisms.
  4. Reduced Integration Complexity – Eliminates the need for multiple point-to-point connections and instead building event driven loosely coupled micro integrations.
  5. Improved Compliance & Security – Ensures HR events reach the right systems while maintaining data integrity.

Conclusion

Event-enabling SuccessFactors with SAP BTP Advanced Event Mesh transforms HR-driven processes by ensuring seamless, real-time integration with enterprise applications. Organizations that adopt this architecture can improve operational efficiency, enhance security, and provide a superior employee experience by keeping all connected systems up to date without delay.

In the next blog in this series, we’ll explore the technical implementation of event-driven SuccessFactors integrations using AEM, Integration Suite, including Intelligent Services Events and REST-based event triggering. Stay tuned!

Are you ready to modernize your SuccessFactors integration with event-driven architecture? Let’s start the conversation!

 

Blog Authors: 

Sumeet Koshal - Solace 

Director of EDA Solutions North America 

connect on linked in 

Cooper Walsh - SAP

Sr. Solutions Advisor - SAP BTP

connect on linked in

entry.title: Re: Use groovy script to extract attachment content from soap message in CPI
entry.content:
Hi I tried sending an attachment over SOAP where in the zip file I attached is having 3 files.  Error I received even though the 3 files are processed is "Outbound processing in endpoint at /readZipFileMulti failed with message "Fault:Could not generate the XML stream caused by: com.ctc.wstx.exc.WstxIOException: Invalid UTF-8 start byte 0x8f (at char #61, byte #-1).", caused by "CharConversionException:Invalid UTF-8 start byte 0x8f (at char #61, byte #-1)".  What I assume problem is when all the files are read this issue occurs.

Refer to the iFLOW

 ReadAttachments: 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.util.Map
import java.util.Iterator
import javax.activation.DataHandler

def Message processData(Message message) {

Map<String, DataHandler> attachments = message.getAttachments()
if (attachments.isEmpty()) {
message.setBody("No attachment Found")
return message;
} else {
Iterator<DataHandler> it = attachments.values().iterator()
DataHandler attachment = it.next()
message.setBody(attachment.getContent())
}
return message
}

Please suggest what is the best way to correct.

Thanks, 
SB

entry.title: Fiori Element - Fragment - Uncaught TypeError
entry.content:
Hi,
I have an oData V4 Service and I'm working with Fiori Elements. Into the App I want to insert an "Custom Section" (Fragment).

I have written the following coding. If the method _pf is called the program gets an Uncaught type error.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
], function(MessageToast) {
    'use strict';

    return {
        onPress: function(oEvent) {
            MessageToast.show("Custom handler invoked.");
            this._pf();
        },
        _pf: function(){
            MessageToast.show("Message2");
        }

    };
});

Does anybody have an idea what the problem is?

entry.title: Fiori where to set default ADAPT FILTERs field
entry.content:
Hi Expert

 

every time I need to click ADAPT FILTERS and add new field. 

Is there any menu where I can set default ADAPT FILTERS field?

pls advise.

THanks in advanceabap

entry.title: Datasphere Data Flow did not import all records
entry.content:
Hello

We have a task chain that runs a data flow which imports a CSV file from an SFTP site.  It has been running weekly without issues, but today it only imported a subset of the rows.  It did not return an error message. 

Has anyone come across this?  Any way to diagnose what happened? 

Thanks

entry.title: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
We have a business requirement to print HU labels after picking and packing. We want to print the label after the material has been committed to the Handling Unit (not at HU creation).

Looking at OPD, I don't see an available option to represent this. It looks like there is a flag for 'HU Task exists'. This should work, as when the material is committed to the HU, it also creates the drop off HU Task. However, this doesn't seem to trigger our outputs.

Is there an OPD trigger for Handling Unit output type to trigger HU label creation at product WT confirmation?

entry.title: Finding Your Voice: The Power of Experience in Public Speaking
entry.content:
Every time you speak in front of an audience—whether in a small meeting, a team presentation, or on a big stage—you gain something valuable. Maybe it’s the realization that pausing can be more powerful than rushing through your words. Or the moment when you connect with your audience, and their engagement fuels your confidence. Perhaps it's learning how to handle unexpected situations, like a technical glitch or a tricky question.

There is no one-size-fits-all approach to becoming a strong speaker. Some of us start with Toastmasters, others take the leap by volunteering for panel discussions, and some find their voice through structured programs like our Speaker Journey. What matters is taking that first step and learning from every experience along the way.

Now, we’d love to hear from you!
What has been your most important experience on your journey to public speaking? Share your story in the comments!

#SAPWomenInTech #PublicSpeaking #FindYourVoice #SpeakerJourney

entry.title: Re: How to consume SAP DataSphere using Google Looker?
entry.content:
Hi Asim,

Thank you for the blog.

Can you please explain a bit more detail around why Precog or Cdata are to be used and what exact technical role are they playing? Are they merely simulating a DB from the ODATA call that can be consumed as a db connection by looker?

Using the free trials of Precog or Cdata are great for experiments but not for enterprise setup without additional cost or security considerations.

Is there a plan on the datashpere roadmap to support looker connections natively?

 

Are there other btp services that can be leveraged that provide the same functionality as Precog or Cdata?

entry.title: Fiori set default view settings
entry.content:
Hi Expert;

 

To change layout of field display on screen on VIEW SETTINGS icon. how to set default for this?

pls advise.

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract - Automatic pricing conditions det...
entry.content:
Dear Hari,

I shouldn’t set a real condition record. I just need to automatically determine a set of pricing conditions with a value of 0. So, a kind of default condition that the user should then update before releasing the Condition Contract. Is there any object I can use for this?

Thank you

Michele

entry.title: SAP Build Apps Functions - "Utility" of all formulas available for Data Transformation
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps is a powerful low-code platform that enables users to create robust applications with minimal coding. One of the key features of SAP Build Apps is its ability to use functions and formulas to manipulate and transform data dynamically. In this blog, we'll explore when to use these functions and how to get the desired output from formulas.

Understanding SAP Build Apps Functions

Functions in SAP Build Apps are predefined operations that can be used to perform specific tasks within your application. These functions can range from simple arithmetic operations to complex data manipulations. Here are some common scenarios where you might need to use functions:

  1. Data Validation: Ensuring that user inputs meet certain criteria before processing.
  2. Data Transformation: Converting data from one format to another, such as changing date formats or calculating derived values.
  3. Conditional Logic: Implementing if-else conditions to control the flow of your application based on specific criteria.

Using the Formula Editor

The Formula Editor in SAP Build Apps is a powerful tool that allows you to create and apply formulas to your data. It combines spreadsheet-like formulas with built-in support for application context, making it easy to create complex algorithms. Here’s how to get started:

Function NameFormulaFormula DescriptionExamples with Result
UtilityBOOLEANConverts the given value to a true/false. This conversion is equivalent to JavaScript BOOLEAN("123")
=true

BOOLEAN(123)
=true

BOOLEAN("")
=false
UtilityDECODE_BASE64Converts a given Base64 encoded text to UTF-8 text.DECODE_BASE64("Zm9vYmFy")
="foobar"

DECODE_BASE64("w6TDpMOk")
="äää"
UtilityDECODE_JSONParses the given text with JSON-formatted content to the value it represents (number, true/false, list or object).DECODE_JSON('"foobar"')
="foobar"

DECODE_JSON('[1, 2, null, "123", true]')
=[1, 2, null, "123", true]

DECODE_JSON('{"a": 1, "b": null, "c": "123", "d": true}')
={"a": 1, "b": null, "c": "123", "d": true}
UtilityDEFAULTIf the given value is non-empty (as per the definitions in the EMPTY  function), returns the given value, otherwise return the given default.DEFAULT(true, 1)
=true

DEFAULT(null, 1)
=1

DEFAULT({ a: 1 }, 1)
={ a: 1 }

DEFAULT([], 1)
=[]
UtilityDEFAULT_FILE_EXTENSIONReturns the default file extension associated with the given MIME type.DEFAULT_FILE_EXTENSION("application/json")
="json"

DEFAULT_FILE_EXTENSION("application/javascript")
="js"

DEFAULT_FILE_EXTENSION("text/plain")
="txt"

DEFAULT_FILE_EXTENSION("video/mp4")
="mp4"

DEFAULT_FILE_EXTENSION("application/octet-stream")
="bin"

UtilityENCODE_BASE64Converts a given UTF-8 text to Base64-encoded text.ENCODE_BASE64("foobar")
=Zm9vYmFy

ENCODE_BASE64("äää")
=w6TDpMOk
UtilityENCODE_FOR_URLURL encodes the given text, escaping any special characters. Returns the encoded text.
ENCODE_FOR_URL("https://example.com/example.html?color=red&width=120")
="https%3A%2F%2Fexample.com%2Fexample.html%3Fcolor%3Dred%26width%3D120"


ENCODE_FOR_URL("https://test.net/test?animal=cat&color=black")
="https%3A%2F%2Ftest.net%2Ftest%3Fanimal%3Dcat%26color%3Dblack"
UtilityENCODE_JSONConverts a given JSON-format input (number, true/false, list, object) into a text.ENCODE_JSON("foobar")
=""foobar""

ENCODE_JSON([1, 2, null, "123", true])
="[1,2,null,"123",true]"

ENCODE_JSON({a: 1, b: null, c: "123", d: true})
="{"a":1,"b":null,"c":"123","d":true}"

ENCODE_JSON([1, 2, null, "123", true], 2)
="[   1,   2,   null,   "123",   true ]"

ENCODE_JSON({a: 1, b: null, c: "123", d: true}, 2)
="{   "a": 1,   "b": null,   "c": "123",   "d": true }"
UtilityENCODE_OBJECT_FOR_URLEncodes an object as key=value pairs joined by an ampersand (&) character. The keys and values are percent encoded. Only keys with string, number or boolean values are included. This format is typically used in URLs to specify query parameters (after the 


ENCODE_OBJECT_FOR_URL({key: "value"})
="key=value"

ENCODE_OBJECT_FOR_URL({sum: 23, currency: "€"})
="sum=23&currency=%E2%82%AC"

ENCODE_OBJECT_FOR_URL({redirect_uri: "https://my-domain.tld/oauth?nonce=abc"})
="redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fmy-domain.tld%2Foauth%3Fnonce%3Dabc"
UtilityFILENAME_IS_IMAGE Return TRUE if the given text is or has a file extension that belongs to a web-compatible image MIME type.FILENAME_IS_IMAGE("a.png")
=true

FILENAME_IS_IMAGE("test.jpg")
=true

FILENAME_IS_IMAGE("/folder/test.best.svg")
=true

FILENAME_IS_IMAGE("video.mp4")
=true
UtilityIFIf the first parameter evaluates to TRUE , returns the second parameter. Otherwise, returns the third parameter.IF(true, "yes", "no")
="yes"

IF(false, "yes", "no")
="no"
UtilityINTEGERConverts the given input value to an integer number. Texts are parsed, and numbers are rounded down. If the input is already an integer, it is returned as-is. Any other values, and invalid texts, result in null ValueINTEGER("123")
=123

INTEGER("123.567")
=123

INTEGER("-0896")
=-896

INTEGER(5.67)
=5

INTEGER("6e-7")
=0
UtilityIS_EMAILReturns TRUE  if the given text is a valid email address, otherwise returns IS_EMAIL("test@mail.com")
=true

IS_EMAIL("@mail.com")
=false
UtilityIS_EMPTYReturns TRUE or FALSE based on if the given value is empty.
Returns TRUE  if the parameter is one of the following values:
an empty text
an empty list		IS_EMPTY("")
=true

IS_EMPTY("a")
=false

IS_EMPTY([])
=true

IS_EMPTY({})
=true

IS_EMPTY(0)
=false
UtilityIS_EQUALReturns TRUE if the both parameter values are equal, otherwise false. . Same than using the ==  operator in formulas.
The values are considered equal if they are of the same type and represent the same value. If the values are objects or arrays, a deep comparison is performed. Objects are considered equal if they have the same properties. Arrays are considered equal if they have equal items in the same order.		IS_EQUAL("cat", "cat")
=true

IS_EQUAL({name: "John"}, {name: "John"})
=true

IS_EQUAL(["cat"], ["cat"])
=true
UtilityIS_NULLReturns TRUE value.  if the given parameter is null value. For any other value, including undefined, returns FALSEIS_NULL(null)
=true

IS_NULL(undefined)
=false

IS_NULL(false)
=false

IS_NULL(0)
=false

IS_NULL("")
=false
UtilityIS_NULLYReturns True  if the given value is either null or undefined.
For any other value, returns false		IS_NULLY(null)
=false

IS_NULLY(undefined)
=true

IS_NULLY(false)
=false

IS_NULLY(0)
=false

IS_NULL("")
=false
UtilityIS_NUMBERReturns whether or not the given value is number.
 This returns TRUE if the parameter type is number.. For any other value this returns FALSE. . Note that this returns false  for numeric texts.		IS_NUMBER(undefined)
=false

IS_NUMBER(null)
=false

IS_NUMBER(false)
=false

IS_NUMBER(0)
=true

IS_NUMBER(1)
=true

IS_NUMBER("3")
=false

IS_NUMBER("")
=false
UtilityIS_SAMEReturn TRUE if the both parameter values are exactly the same, otherwise FALSE . Same than using the === operator in formulas.IS_SAME("cat", "cat")
=true

IS_SAME(2, 2)
=true

IS_SAME({name: "John"}, {name: "John"})
=false

IS_SAME(["cat"], ["cat"])
=false
UtilityIS_UNDEFINEDReturn TRUE if the parameter is undefined value. For all other values, including null this returns
Similar to IS_EQUAL except that it does not perform deep comparison for objects and lists. Instead objects are considered "same" only if they refer to the instance in the memory. In most cases, you might want to use IS_EQUAL  instead of this function.The functionality of this function is equal to JavaScript's 		IS_UNDEFINED(undefined)
=true

IS_UNDEFINED(null)
=false

IS_UNDEFINED(false)
=false

IS_UNDEFINED(0)
=false

IS_UNDEFINED("")
=false
UtilityMIME_TYPEReturns the MIME type of the given file extension, or null  if it cannot be determined. Also works with full paths and filenames, where it's assumed the text ends in an extension.MIME_TYPE("json")
="application/json"

MIME_TYPE(".json")
="application/json"

MIME_TYPE("/cachedImages/test.png")
="image/png"

MIME_TYPE("README.md")
="text/markdown"
UtilityMIME_TYPE_IS_IMAGEReturn TRUE  if the given MIME type belongs to a web-safe image format, and FALSE otherwiseMIME_TYPE_IS_IMAGE("image/apng")
=true

MIME_TYPE_IS_IMAGE("image/png")
=true

MIME_TYPE_IS_IMAGE("image/svg+xml")
=true

MIME_TYPE_IS_IMAGE("video/mp4")
=false

MIME_TYPE_IS_IMAGE("wrong MIME type")
=false
UtilityNOTReturn TRUE if the given parameter is false , otherwise returns FALSE . Equivalent to the  ! OperatorNOT(false)
=true

NOT(true)
=false
UtilityNUMBERConverts the value to a number, if it is not already a number. Numeric texts are parsed. Any other values, and invalid texts, result in null valueNUMBER("123")
=123

NUMBER("-0896")
=-0896

NUMBER(-4.8)
=-4.8
UtilityNUMERIC_BASEConverts the given number into a text representation in another base.
NUMERIC_BASE(123, 2)
="111101"

NUMERIC_BASE(123, 4)
="1323"

NUMERIC_BASE(123, 😎
="173"

NUMERIC_BASE(123, 10)
="123"

NUMERIC_BASE(123, 16)
="7b"

NUMERIC_BASE(123, 2.5)
="111101"

NUMERIC_BASE(123.625, 2)
="111101.101"

NUMERIC_BASE(-123.625, 2)
="-111101.101"
UtilitySTRINGConverts the given value to a text. If the value is a number, returns its text representation, possibly including decimals. True/false values are converted to either TRUE or FALSE. A null  value is converted to an empty text.STRING(123.456)
="123.456"

STRING(true)
="true"


STRING("hello")
="hello"
Utility

URL

Converts the given text to an URL-type text. Returns null  if the text cannot be converted to an URL.URL("http://foo.com/blah_blah")
="http://foo.com/blah_blah"

URL("https://www.example.com/foo/?bar=baz&inga=42&quux")
="https://www.example.com/foo/?bar=baz&inga=42&quux"

URL("http://userid@www.example.com/")
="http://userid@www.example.com/"


URL("http://142.42.1.1:8080/")
= "http://142.42.1.1:8080/"

I will continue to write a next blog with New function and New formulas, Keep watching the blog...

Conclusion

Using functions and formulas in SAP Build Apps can significantly enhance the functionality and user experience of your applications. By understanding when and how to use these tools, you can efficiently transform data and implement complex logic without extensive coding. Experiment with different functions and formulas to see how they can best serve your application needs.

THanks in advance.

abap

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest Winners Revealed
entry.content:
Thrilled to be one of the Winner.

From winning & wearing 2021 Devtoberfest T-shirt to 2024 Final Winner list is a huge leap of progress in learning & collaboration to the SAP community .

Happy Learning & Waiting for next TechEd meet 🙂

entry.title: Re: Tracking of CO2 emissions in SAP
entry.content:
Hello Julia,

I assume you refer to CO2e (carbon dioxide equivalent) as a unit for GHG emissions and not to CO2 as one of the GHGases.

A quick and dirty solution may be to create a characteristic in the MM classification (001). I’m not sure if this will satisfy your sustainability team. Providing CO2e values exclusively is only sufficient for simplified reporting, internal monitoring, benchmarking, or supply chain communication.

Some regulations (e.g., the EU Green Deal, the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), and the U.S. SEC climate disclosure rules) require reporting specific gases separately, and the GHG Protocol encourages detailed tracking of emissions per gas.

CO₂e values depend on Global Warming Potential (GWP) factors, which can change over time. Storing the original gas breakdown allows recalculations if updated GWP values support more accurate carbon accounting. However, it is also insufficient if you are in specific industries like agriculture and food, manufacturing, or refrigeration.

Another more sophisticated method is Specification Management, where complex property trees can build relationships and document dependencies.  This probably only makes sense if your organization uses SAP PLM or EHS. I would not activate Spec Mgmt only for the carbon footprint.

SAP provides several areas where it has already incorporated the carbon footprint. Procurement, for example, can already ask for the detailed carbon footprint of a material in the purchase requisition to make a purchase decision. I’m sure you know SAP’s sustainability solutions around corporate and product carbon footprint calculation processes, which should also be considered.

Let me know if this has helped.

Klaus

entry.title: Re: Restrictions in app Profit Centers - Actuals
entry.content:
Hi Gabor5

I'm sharing with you the following blogs of my colleague George Yu regarding restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

  • Using Restrictions to Enhance User Authorizations in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
  • Precise Control of Fiori App Access with the App Authorization Variants

You can find there all the details and information on How to apply the restrictions by object or by apps

 

Best regards

Edna.

entry.title: Mongo ODBC Connection - failing to connect, Crystal 2020 (ver 14.3.4.5181)
entry.content:
When attempting to connect via ODBC connection to Mongo DB, Crystal 2020 closes without displaying an error.  The windows event viewer shows the following error messages:

.NET Runtime Error

Application: crw64.exe
Framework Version: v4.0.30319
Description: The process was terminated due to an unhandled exception.
Exception Info: exception code c0000005, exception address 00007FFEC0AB2808
Stack:

Application Error

Faulting application name: crw64.exe, version: 14.3.4.5181, time stamp: 0x678025da
Faulting module name: atsql.dll, version: 1.4.2.0, time stamp: 0x675af79d
Exception code: 0xc0000005
Fault offset: 0x0000000000e22808
Faulting process id: 0x2198
Faulting application start time: 0x1DB824F72F1A81F
Faulting application path: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\crw64.exe
Faulting module path: C:\Program Files\MongoDB\Atlas SQL ODBC Driver\bin\atsql.dll
Report Id: 9da99665-65fc-4834-8dd3-593e09ed5510
Faulting package full name:
Faulting package-relative application ID:

(I am able to connect to the DB via MS Excel)

entry.title: Re: Does Basic Syntax support the Format function?
entry.content:
The equivalent function in Crystal is Cstr() or ToText().

The online help provides excellent details.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community CWB no Business Round: Conectando Inovação e Tecnologia no Vale do Pinhão
entry.content: Muito bom saber que vários encontros e eventos estão acontecendo na Comunidade em Curitiba!

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Compensation Introduction & Overview - Part 2
entry.content:
Hi @ravitejapaluri ,

  • We were not able to completely avoid forms or launching them, but here is what we did:
  • We adjusted the route map to be "Comp. Adm. Review -> Comp. Adm. Review Final -> Completed
  • Because we didn't want to confuse managers (planners), using "Text Replacement", we replaced the "Form" Text with a dot (it would not allow us to leave it blank) & replaced the "Executive Review" text with the text "Compensation Planning". 
  • And then granted access to the Executive Review through Role Based Permissions. Created the role with the permissions needed and then assign the role to a group we created based on Pay Grade. 
  • In addition, we created a custom card in the Home Page that had a direct link to the Comp. module so users can go directly to the module. 
  • Once you launch forms, all planners (from the permission group) can access the Comp. Tool through "Compensation Planning" (A.K.A "Executive Review)
  • We granted and removed access through the Permission Group (example: first all planners have access to read and edit; then planners on grade "XYZ" can "read only", but not do edits; and so on) **MSS Role is read only***

The benefit of doing this is that if you use EC, any supervisor changes that occur and terminations are dynamic, and budget gets adjusted immediately vs. when you have forms.

We are still in the cycle and once we close, I can share some lessons learned. 

Hope this helps!

Karla

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Monte Carlo Simulation

Hi @ChenNee ,

Thank you so much for your quick reply.

Kind regards,

Mick

entry.title: Analytics Cloud Connetion to S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hey all

Im currently Facing an issue, while setting up the Live Connetion from an SAC to my S4 Public Cloud.

I have done the setup according to this guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/0485d540e0b340a0bc0da86fe368997c.html?locale=en

While now saving the Live Connection is SAC, i can see the following error within the Developer Tools:

 

 

invalid_grant,
Provided authorization grant is invalid.Exception was The assertion has already been consumed.For more information,
consult the kernel traces or the OAuth 2.0 trouble shooting SAP note 1688545

 

 

Within the Connectivity Trace i have the following Data:

 

Does anyone have an Idea, on what could be the Problem?

Best Regards 🙂

entry.title: SAP Investment Management Future State
entry.content: With the introduction of SAP EPPM for major capital projects, what is the future of SAP Investment Management (IM)? At the PPM bucket level, planning and forecasting can be done at a high level, allowing users to create a portfolio item and trigger projects in both PPM and PS. While PPM offers review mechanisms and scoring models for better decision-making, IM remains an efficient tool for managing budgets. Does SAP recommend completely eliminating IM in favor of EPPM, regardless of user requirements? Or is there still a strategic role for IM in budget management alongside EPPM?

entry.title: Is transposing columns to rows missing in the SAP Analytics Cloud new model type?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP Analytics Cloud new model type (the first option in the "Create Model" popup: "Start with Data", unckeck "Use first row as column headers"), the "Transpose Columns to Rows" button is not visible. It seems this model type does not support it.

If it is not supported yet, how can we perform the transposition in this case?

Thanks!

entry.title: Classification of wage types in the RPLRECQ0 Run to Run Reconciliation Report (Australia)
entry.content:
I would be grateful if someone could help me to understand how wage types are classified between Earnings & Allowances, Retro/Claim from prv, Full Income Tax and Deductions in RPLRCQ0 Run to Run Reconciliation Report (Australia).

It doesn't seem to be the wage type group (T52D7) or evaluation class 05 wage type allocation for reporting.

Many thanks 🙂

I would like to know the steps in any system as I am reading conflicting messages. During tracing my steps, the field is never called.
We are not implementing SAP HR.

Thanx
Corne

Define Poll Enrich | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: BW 7.5 upgrade with S/4 upgrade. Use of ODP to source CDS views
entry.content:
Hi All,

Step 1: AS IS state. Source system : ECC . BW 7.0 system on MS SQL. Using SAPI .

Step 2: to_be interim state. Source system : ECC .... upgrade to BW 7.5 system on MS SQL. Using SAPI

Step 3: Change source system to S/4 HANA.... BW 7.5 system on MS SQL .

We have identified a list of deprecated datasources in S/4. We are finding out CDS views in S/4 that will replace these deprecated objects for example Equipment_attr, employee_attr etc.

How do we connect these CDS views via RSA2 to BW? is it possible with SAPI? or Do we have to mandatorily use ODP? Can we use ODP and SAPI  in parallel for same s/4 system? (the deprecated datasources use ODP and other datasources stay AS IS using SAPI.)

Anybody with experience in such a situation.

 

Regards,

Ratish

 

Regards,

Ratish

entry.title: Transportation lane has multiple means of transport
entry.content:
Spoiler
 

HI All,

In the following scenario, multiple Means of Transport are linked to a lane. All have the same carriers assigned. The difference between them is just Trsp Duration. The system always picks the second Means of transport. We would like to know if there is logic or determination logic which means of transport needs to be determined 

Thanks

Satish

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to export a excle file to local path?
entry.content: In our environment, the files uploaded by users are stored in a dedicated file server, and the path for storing the files is automatically generated by the server. This path is complex and follows no discernible pattern, making it very difficult to retrieve the files using a program. Therefore, we want to handle the file export ourselves and specify the path where the exported files are stored, so as to facilitate further processing of the files by our program. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Transportation lane has multiple means of transport
entry.content:
Go to your Planning profile --> 'Constraints and costs settings' tab.

Go to 'Utilization Settings' section. Check the below 2 fields for all the Means of Transport assigned to the Lane.

1. Minimum Target Utilization in Percent

2. Accept Freight Orders Below Target Utilization

Compare & analyze these fields for all the Means of Transport in the Lane. Your query should be resolved.

entry.title: Re: Asset Master Data & Asset Purchasing in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content: Really nice insight!

entry.title: Re: How to detect / find out which SLT version is installed
entry.content:
1. In SAPGUI, Go to System Menu and select Status ...
  2. Click on magnifying glass icon under "SAP System data" pane
  3. Click on "Installed Product Versions"
  4. Your SLT Release and SP level will be listed here (like SLT 3.0 SP 12)

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Chennai, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP-based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday 10th April 2025 in Chennai, India from 9:00 - 16:00 IST. 

Location: Business Core Solutions ,No 162, Essar Construction, Kundrathur High Road, Gerugambakkam, Chennai 600122

The event is kindly being hosted by Business Core Solutions, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page.

The instructor is Developer Advocate Shilpa Shankar. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Data Access Control in SAP Datasphere - Operator and values DAC
entry.content: @nkulkarni It is the other way around. Same restriction values for the 'AND' and different restriction values are 'OR'

entry.title: Re: Fiori where to set default ADAPT FILTERs field
entry.content:
Hi，
You can save your prefer the filter via the View management like below shows.
Please note if the filter name contains '*' it means current changing does not save.

Best regards,

Jing

entry.title: SAP 分析云经典模型和故事迁移指南
entry.content:
由于SAP 分析云经典故事计划将于2026年第二季度QRC 更新后停用（相关博客），我们鼓励所有SAP 分析云租户中仍存在大量经典故事和模型的客户开始计划并尝试将这些经典故事迁移至优化故事的同时，也将经典模型迁移至新模型这一话题纳入考量。虽然SAP 分析云中提供了一些迁移工具，但考虑到实际业务环境的复杂性，我们建议您为迁移预留充足的时间，以减轻迁移带来的业务影响。

迁移前需要考虑并回答的主要问题：

1. 迁移范围（What）：本次迁移的主要内容有哪些？

在迁移之前，需要确定主要迁移的内容，例如：此次迁移是仅进行经典故事的迁移还是也有数字化董事会议厅内容的迁移；是将所有经典故事都进行迁移，还是仅迁移一些常用的故事；此次迁移是否需要一并将经典模型的迁移也纳入进来。在我们已知的客户实践中，有些客户的SAP 分析云系统中全部使用的是经典账户模型，考虑到新模型的功能优势：性能提升、增强的货币换算、更灵活的计算等（相关博客），以及新模型的战略地位和客户自身的预算限制，他们会将经典模型的迁移与经典故事的迁移打包在一起进行，以节约成本并减少业务影响。在迁移前明确迁移范围是十分必要的，因为这将对后面迁移时间和迁移参与人员的预估产生重要影响。

2. 迁移时间（When）：什么时候开始迁移？需要多长时间进行迁移？

在明确了迁移范围之后，企业需要根据实际的业务情况对迁移时间进行预估、对迁移流程进行合理的分配与安排，并留出充足的时间对迁移后的内容进行检查和试运行。

3. 参与人员（Who）：执行迁移的主要人员有哪些？迁移过程中需要哪些人员的配合？

在明确了迁移范围及时间之后，企业需要根据实际情况安排迁移的执行人员，在有条件的情况下对相关人员进行迁移的培训，并提前告知相关部门以配合迁移。

推荐的整体迁移步骤（How）：

1. 模型迁移：此步骤为非必要步骤，具体要根据企业划定的迁移范围来确定是否需要。用户可以使用建模区域中的“迁移为新模型类型”按钮将经典账户模型转换为新模型类型，该项迁移暂时不支持批量操作。以经典账户模型为例，迁移后的模型中会添加一个度量，自动设置为SignedData，用户可自行调整。模型的计算指标会自动设置，无需再进行额外操作。

模型中的“迁移为新模型类型”按钮

迁移前 - 经典账户模型的结构

迁移后 - 新模型的结构

一些需要注意的事项（相关帮助文档）：

• 迁移无法撤销。

• 如果经典账户模型已经具有相关对象（包括故事、数据操作、私有版本等）或启用了货币换算时无法进行迁移，同时为保证现有内容的可用性，请务必复制出一个模型，保证复制出的模型没有相关对象并禁用其货币换算，然后对复制的模型进行迁移。在迁移完成后再决定是否将货币换算开启，并在迁移后的模型中重新定义货币换算度量。

• 新模型类型的数据导入/导出作业与经典账户模型的旧导入/数据导入作业不兼容，因此在迁移后，必须为新模型类型创建新的数据导入/导出作业。

• 将经典账户模型迁移到新模型类型时，不会迁移数据点留言。

2. 故事迁移：请注意，优化故事体验并不包含经典故事中目前支持的所有功能，这些使用限制计划将逐渐被移除或改进。因此在进行故事迁移前，请阅读此关于优化故事体验的限制。该项迁移支持批量操作，请阅读此博客以获取更多批量迁移相关的详情。由于故事迁移具有不可逆性，同时也为了保证报告本身的访问权限不需要重新设置，请务必复制出一份故事副本作为备份，并直接在原有报告上进行迁移。

故事批量转换工具的入口

单一故事转换按钮

在经典设计体验中，故事中可能会既有网格页面，也有画布或响应式页面。由于网格页面在新的“优化设计体验”中不再支持，所以网格页面的整体迁移过程包括：

1) 用优化故事体验新建原网格页面中的报表

2) 在经典故事中删除网格页面，然后将其他页面先转换为“优化设计体验”

3) 将步骤2) 中转换后的故事和步骤1) 中的内容进行整合

3. 切换模型依赖项的模型：此步骤为非必要步骤，若进行了模型迁移则该步骤为必要步骤。此处的模型依赖项主要包括故事、数据操作等跟模型绑定的对象。在故事迁移完成之后，迁移后的故事及故事中的数据操作等内容依旧是与经典模型进行绑定的，则此时我们需要将这些对象中的经典模型替换成新模型。SAP 分析云支持在故事中将“经典账户模型”替换为为同样结构的“含账户和度量的新模型”。故事的模型替换方法及注意事项，您可以参阅此博客。数据操作的模型替换，可以直接在数据操作中进行更改，并且数据操作的模型替换允许撤销，但为保证报表的正常应用，我们仍建议备份相关的数据操作。

在故事中替换模型

在数据操作中替换模型

4. 故事的整合：此步骤为非必要步骤，需要根据实际情况判断是否需要进行。需要进行故事整合的一些用户案例：

• 经典故事中既有网格页面，又有画布或响应式页面；

• 有数字化董事会议厅内容，并且该内容由不同的经典故事组成；

• 故事的查看者期望通过一个统一的入口查看不同的故事内容；

故事的整合有两种基本的方式，一种是通过复制粘贴来实现（SAP 帮助文档），另一种是通过导入故事页面来实现（SAP 帮助文档）。通过复制粘贴，用来复制的原始故事跟粘贴后的目标故事是两个独立的内容，也就是说，在原始故事上的更改无法同步到目标故事；而通过导入故事页面，原始故事上的修改可以被SAP 分析云识别，用户可以在左侧面板中的“大纲”项下选择将内容同步。您可以根据企业的实际业务需要，在这两种方式当中进行选择。

在复杂的企业实践当中，故事的迁移是一个牵一发而动全身的项目。在阅读本文后，如果您对迁移还有疑问或者还需要我们提供哪些方面的帮助和支持，您可以在评论区进行留言。

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content: Very Informative Alex..Thanks for sharing

entry.title: Re: How to Add Custom Fields to Supplier Invoice Template Using Coding Block Business Context?
entry.content:
Hello PChacon,

You can use the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules app to create validation rules for the new fields. Note that substitution/validation for the Coding Block business context is only possible for certain types of account, and the available fields that can be substituted and validated depends on the context you select. For detailed information, please refer to the following documentation on SAP Help Portal: Rules for Substitution/Validation | SAP Help Portal .

entry.title: @capabilities.DeleteRestriction can't use the Dynamic Expressions, it has no effect
entry.content:
Hi，expert！

I use annotations to control the property 'stock' of this entity, when its value is equal to 0 can be deleted, but it does not work, I check the value of not 0 in my table, still can be deleted, could you tell my code is wrong?

it is my code:

entity BooksAnalytics : managed
{
    key ID : Integer
        @title : 'ID';
    title : String
        @title : 'Title';
    stock : Integer
        @title : 'Stock';
    category1 : String
        @title : 'Category1';
    category2 : String
        @title : 'Category2';
    publishedAt : Date
        @title : 'Published At';
    authors : Association to one Authors
        @title : 'Author';
}......
//动态删除操作 动态表达式
annotate BooksAnalytics with @(
    Capabilities.DeleteRestrictions: {
        $Type: 'Capabilities.DeleteRestrictionsType',
        Deletable: 
            {$edmJson: {$If: [{$Eq: [{$Path: 'stock'}, 0]}, true,false]}}
        
    }
);

and there is the xml:

it still can be deleted when it’s not equal 0

entry.title: How to add GHS data into SAP?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I need to add GHS data into SAP, but I’m not sure where to start.

Could you please help me with the following questions?

  • Where can I find the list of available GHS entries?
  • How do I add them to the system?
  • What configurations or settings are required?

Any guidance or documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Issue: Data Not Displaying on CDS Endpoint Despite Presence in HANA Table
entry.content: Hi @martinstenzig Contributor, Thank you for your question! Initially, I was running the application locally using "cds watch --profile hybrid", and I noticed that the OData results were not visible. After debugging, I found that the issue was caused by the SuccessFactors API call. I was using the HTTP request method to fetch data from SuccessFactors, which was interfering with the retrieval of data from the HANA tables simultaneously. I appreciate your insight—it helped me investigate the issue more effectively. Thank you! 😊

entry.title: Re: What do SAP specialists do when they get old?
entry.content:
A decade old answer but what's jotted here is so relevant! and it would remain always. Am sure this answer is going to age like a fine 🍷.

I actually enjoyed reading this and in parallel enlightened me for focusing on what's next since am also in the same juncture now and I must say this read was pretty helpful.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: When executing Odata API of S/4 with POST method,405 error is returned.
entry.content:
Hello,

Did you find any solution for this? I have same issue for -API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV.

entry.title: Re: SAP MM - INTERCOMPANY (CROSS COMPANY)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"Intercompany, refers to transactions between different company codes within the same corporate group. These transactions involve the sale, purchase, or transfer of materials between entities that are legally separate but under the same organization."

Thanks for your sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Deployment of html5 application content failed on cloud foundry while deploying the mta app
entry.content: Thank you so much!

entry.title: Re: Is transposing columns to rows missing in the SAP Ana...
entry.content:
Hi @pedro_rangel ,

I tried same scenario with data model but its not worked you can go with 2nd option.

Or  you have to do it in excel then go with 1st option

Here is the step for empty model transpose option

  1. Go with 2nd option start with empty. create dimensions and measures

 

  1. Upload your input file
  2. In data wrangling steps you have option for transpose

 

 

  1. First select column those you want to transpose using control key

 

  1. Select highlighted area Unpivot in pic 1
  2. Click on ok
  3. After transpose your table will look like this

 

 

Just save and map to dimensions and measures in the next step.

 

Kindly upvote! 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Curious to know if SAP is selling fresh licences of S...
entry.content: Hi Dominik, Thanks for the response. I understand, but still, trying to understand till which year IN THE PAST, it is available.

entry.title: Issue with Loading Chart When Using Linked Dimensions in SAC
entry.content:
I am facing an issue with a chart in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). The chart loads successfully when using data from a file directly. However, when I try to use a linked dimension from another dataset, the chart only shows a loading indicator and does not display any data.

Here are some additional details:

  • The dimension and measure data are correctly set up.
  • Network connectivity is fine, and no errors are visible in the browser console.
  • The issue persists even after checking the chart configuration and verifying the data in the linked dimension.
  • No error message is appearing, and the chart simply fails to load.

I have checked all configurations, but the issue remains. Could anyone assist with troubleshooting this problem?

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Node.js - Insert vector embedding data into h...
entry.content:
The following change would get you going with cap-js/hana

 

> embedding: array2VectorBuffer(embedding),
----
< embedding: JSON.stringify(embedding),

 

You could ask for supporting binary representation at https://github.com/cap-js/cds-dbs/issues

PS: I see that native queries support binary representation. You could try that too and let us know if that works better than text representation. 🙂

entry.title: Re: question regarding S/4 HANA Cloud Backups
entry.content: Hi, ok, but how about human mistakes? What if some angry accountant deleted hundred of documents and thus made tenant inoperative? Does SAP support just one tenant restore?

entry.content: Thanks for this solution!

entry.title: To generate a QR code in SAP ABAP that includes special characters
entry.content: I am creating a program in SAP using Adobe Forms and a QR code generator. I have the following code: 0106933265514620215-&RWoH3Y-.Tk91FFD092dGVzdApr7bADFkbQnxVk86w/obSPK=QDQDQ. I want to display this code in a QR code, but I encountered an issue where special characters are being replaced with a # sign instead of the ASCII Group Separator. How can I generate the QR code correctly in SAP ABAP?

entry.title: BYD2502 OutboundDeliveryRuns__ScheduleJobFail
entry.content:
Run job failed with error "Cancelled manually or by the system".


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2502 OutboundDeliveryRuns__ScheduleJobFail
entry.content:
Error reported user has been locked in this tenant.

Follow the below steps to unlock User:

  1. Navigate to Application and User Management work center.
  2. Select Business User view under User and Access Management tab.
  3. In search field, search for user ID.
  4. Click Unlock User button.

Then the Run will work as normal.

3. From the link, check if the hardware requirements are met or not

https://documentation.suse.com/sles-sap/15-SP5/html/SLES-SAP-guide/cha-plan.html

Regards

SS

entry.title: Show auto derived Quarter in OP from Input Key field on clicking CREATE button in LROP?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a List Report Object Page app using CAP Fiori Elements application with CRUD functionality. Once I click on CREATE Button in List Report Page, a Popup with Key field Date appears where I enter a Date from value help and click continue which shows Object Page with multiple fields maintained in Facets. I have read only fields like Year, Month and Quarter which must be derived automatically from Date entered in popup in CAP Fiori Element LROP app.

Can anyone suggest me how to achieve the auto filled values into Year, Month & Quarter fields from Date key fields maintained in popup.

Thank you very much in advance.

Regards,

Ramana.

entry.title: Re: REFX integration with PM/ FSM
entry.content:
Hello Sayeesh,

Based on your email I'm assuming you are using the Usage View in RE-FX. Here you can use the integration option to PM to link the relevant RE objects to Functional Locations (FL) in PM. You can do this manually or use the integration option to automate this process whereby you might have to implement a BAdI for specific logic.

Maintenance costs are never recorded in RE-FX and are either captured via MM or in PM using the MM integration in case of external contractors. In PM you will likely have a FL for each RO and in PM you normally create lower level equipment objects for things like aircon, and other technical objects that require maintenance). You should always create the work order on the lowest level for accurate cost collection and reporting and in PM you will be able to roll up the costs on the higher level FL to give you a view per RO, BU or BE.

If you don't have to charge back the maintenance costs to the tenant there would be not further configuration involved.

Regards, Andre

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Tools Download from SAP Software Download Ce...
entry.content:
Hello marcelfrank,

Please refer to below SAP documents about how to download and install each tools.

[HANA Studio]
2902153 - How to download SAP HANA Studio? https://me.sap.com/notes/2902153

[ADT] 2496400 - How to download ABAP Development Tools and install it. https://me.sap.com/notes/2496400

[BWMT] 1954169 - BWMT - Installation details https://me.sap.com/notes/1954169

Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Re: Capacity Planning
entry.content:
Hello @DafinaZahra  ,

First of all , your blogging is excellent. I read your previous blogs. Thanks for the sharing valuable information.

Regarding Capacity Planning, it is useful tool to evaluate and enhanced production capacity of work centers for production planning. I would say without capacity planning we cannot achieve full optimization of PP module in SAP.

I will request you to please check below community blogs on capacity planning. In case of specific query, post in comment section, experts will try to answer you as much as possible.

Capacity Planning (Leveling) and Evaluation in S4H... - SAP Community

Introduction to Capacity Requirement planning in S... - SAP Community

Exploring Capacity Planning

SAP Capacity Planning

In case of provided answer is enough for you then you can close this thread.

entry.title: Display Line Chart in ALP Visual Filter using String field Quarter(2024Q1) in SAP CAP CDS FE?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a SAP CAP ALP Fiori Element app using CDS (NodeJS) with Visual Filters. I'm able to display Bar chart with length of Dimension must be 4 and above that chart is not showing the values. Also I have field Quarter with length 6 and format is 2024Q1/2024Q2 i.e. YYYYQ1/2/3/4. Also I have added annotation as Common.IsFiscalYearQuarter: true which will help to show Line Chart for String field also. But at present V4 Fiori Elements app is not supporting.

Can anyone help me to show Charts other than Bar chart as Visual Filters in SAP CAP CDS Fiori Element ALP app using NodeJS.

Thank you in advance.

Regards,

Ramana.

entry.title: Re: Failed to subscribe Build Apps in BTP Trial
entry.content:
Hi All. 
I'm not sure if this problem is the same for everyone, but I was able to access the SAP Build App through BTP successfully.
Here’s what I did—hope it helps!
1, Create Global account with region US East instead of Singapore.
※It doesn't work well with Singpore server after several times tried.

2, In step to trigure SAP Build App at Booster (Finish step 5 Review).
An email of activate for Identity Authentication Service is sent to your email. 
Access activate link, and choose Applications  Resources.
I enabled below Applications and was able to access SAP Build app.

Thank you

Kind regards,

Mick
